M.description: Total number of candidate hospital sites (including existing hospitals)

a.description: Coverage indicator: 1 if household i is within distance S of hospital j, 0 otherwise

p.description: Maximum number of new hospitals that can be opened

assumptions.description: Number of household clusters is positive.
Total number of candidate hospital sites is positive.
Population of each household cluster is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(v[i] >= 0 for i in range(nI))

assumptions.description: Maximum number of new hospitals is non-negative.
Number of existing hospitals does not exceed total hospital sites.

x.description: 1 if hospital j is opened, 0 otherwise

y.description: 1 if household i is served by at least one open hospital within distance S, 0 otherwise

constraints.description: All existing hospitals must remain open.
At most p new hospitals can be opened.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(gp.quicksum(x[j] for j in range(m, M)) <= p)

constraints.description: A household is covered only if at least one open hospital within range is open.
constraints.formulation: y_i \leq \sum_{j \in J} a_{ij} x_j \quad \forall i \in I

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(y[i] <= gp.quicksum(a[i][j] * x[j] for j in range(M)) for i in range(nI))

objective.description: Maximize the total population with access to a hospital within the maximum allowed travel distance.

code.gurobipy:
model.setObjective(
    gp.quicksum(v[i] * y[i] for i in range(nI)),
    GRB.MAXIMIZE
)
